fix: surface API-fetch failures with CloudOff indicator
Mirror the existing GPS-stale top-bar indicator for the data path. When a
refresh cycle's per-stop departure-board fetches all fail (typical when
the device drops off the network), the UI used to keep showing stale rows
with no signal that anything was wrong. The dispatched dispatch was
visible only via the logs surfaced in reports.
Adds a `dataStale: StateFlow<Boolean>` to both `NearbyViewModel` and
`DepartureBoardViewModel`, set when every fetched name returns null (or
the single-stop call throws); the new `RefreshCountdownIndicator.dataStale`
parameter renders `Icons.Filled.CloudOff` next to the existing
`LocationOff` slot. Partial failures don't trigger it.
Drive-by: `buildPredictedDepartures` was reading `LocalDate.now(zone)` for
the anchor — a hidden dependency on system clock that contradicted the
function's nominal pure-input signature. Derive the anchor day from
`nowMs` instead.

fix(trip-view): anchor schedule by trip, not by stop
The route view computed each stop's epoch ms independently against today's
calendar date with a one-shot "≥12h ahead → subtract a day" patch. Stops
whose clock time crosses midnight (e.g. opening the view at 23:50 with
later stops at 00:02) collapsed into early-morning of *today*, breaking
the trip's monotonic ordering. The estimator then walked the list and
locked the active dot to a phantom stop placed ~24h in the past.
Replace the per-stop computation with a trip-wide anchor: the first stop
is placed on whichever calendar day puts it within ±12h of now; from
there we walk forward and advance the day every time a stop's
time-of-day appears to step backwards relative to its predecessor. The
list is monotonic by construction, regardless of midnight crossings or
GTFS 24+ extended-form strings (`25:30`).
`computeStopCountdown` now reads the pre-anchored ms instead of
re-parsing per stop, which also removes the latent risk of double-
applying delay in the row.
Drops WhatsApp from .github/notify.txt; future `` runs go to
Telegram only.

fix: nearby cap, split-mode order, retryable report, dev annotations
- Nearby `stopsToShow` cap is now enforced inside the mode-group loop, so
  split mode no longer overshoots the configured count by one
  (`BuildNearbyResultUseCase`).
- Split-mode groups within a stop are ordered metro → train → tram → bus →
  ferry by importance instead of relying on `groupBy` iteration order
  (`GroupDeparturesByModeUseCase`).
- Report screen keeps the Submit button visible on Error and shows the error
  text alongside it, so a failed submit is retryable without losing typed
  fields (`ReportScreen`).
- Developer route view: drop the `±5` gate around the active stop on
  arrive/depart annotation buttons; the buttons now appear on every stop in
  developer mode (`TripDetailSheet`).
- New regression tests for the cap-in-split-mode and split-mode ordering.
- Bumps to 1.10.3 (versionCode 94).

- UpdateChecker — owns updateState + cool-off + checkForUpdate / setDownloading
- TrackedReportsRepository — owns trackedReports / hasResolvedReports / dismiss

Removed the Upload APK artifact step. The same APK and AAB are already
attached to the GitHub Release as assets, and the release-publisher
dispatch reads from the release, so the Actions artifact was redundant
and was hitting GitHub's storage quota — blocking v1.9.5 from cutting
a release.
Added a Prune old workflow artifacts step at the start of the build
that keeps the newest 3 artifacts and deletes the rest. Defensive
against any future workflow that uploads artifacts.
Bumps to v1.9.6 since v1.9.5 never published; the artifact-quota
failure prevented Create Release from running.
fix(nearby): per-mode distance + per-mode estimator [release][publish]
Two related improvements to the Nearby screen and trip detail sheet.
Nearby distance per mode group: when grouping is off, each mode row
(e.g. "Olovnice - Bus" vs "Olovnice - Train") now shows the distance
to the closest platform serving that mode, computed from the API
platformLocations response. Falls back to the cache distance when GPS
or platform locations are unavailable. Also refines distance for
grouped stops since a name can span platforms hundreds of meters apart.
Vehicle position estimator parameterized by transport type: dwell and
transition durations now scale with mode (longer dwells for trains,
faster transitions for metro). Threaded TransportType through
TripDetail, TripDetailController, and the minimized state so restore
preserves the value. Replay grid script updated to match.
fix(ui): align route numbers in departure rows
Fixed min width on route number text so single, double, and triple
digit route names align headsign text consistently.
fix(nearby): sort favorite mode groups first in split stops (#86)
API calls deduplicated by stop name. Mode groups sorted so favorited
key appears before non-favorited siblings. All checks use exact key
comparison — no base-name fallbacks.

refactor(scanner): dedup MindAR helpers in main.js
Three near-identical idioms appeared multiple times each:
- 'document.querySelector("body > video")' (5×) → getArVideo()
- 'if (mindARSys) { try { mindARSys.stop() } catch ... }' (2×) → stopMindAR()
- remove + rAF re-add 'fade-in' class (2×) → restartArVideoFadeIn()
Also tighten the anim picker: replace the three-element for-of fallback
with a one-line find(), and document why the allowlist matters
(unknown names must not pass through to the dynamic import or the
URL could load an arbitrary module path).

ci: migrate to release-publisher composite action
The publisher now ships dispatch/action.yml that owns recipient
encryption + asset collection + dispatch POST. Drop the locally-forked
copy (scripts/encrypt_recipients.py + tests) — there's only one
canonical wire format and it lives upstream.
Pass set-web-url: true + web-url so the publisher records pexesongy's
hosted URL on its app card. The publisher otherwise leaves apps[].webUrl
untouched between releases.
Also drops the cryptography dev dep we added — the action installs it
itself and tests no longer need it.
refactor: small cleanups in bump_version and print/generate
- bump_version: use os.environ | {...} instead of {**os.environ, ...}
- print/generate: drop a duplicated cards-per-sheet guard; the second
  guard subsumes the first
docs: clarify targets.mind precondition in Quick Start
A fresh-clone reader followed Quick Start expecting the scanner to
work after build.py — but build.py only writes cards.json + art/, and
targets.mind is a separately-baked binary. Note that the committed
copy works out of the box and recompile is only required when build.py
warns about a compile-order change.
refactor(scanner): centralise error formatting in lib/error.js
Pull the repeated 'String(err.message || err).slice(0, 50)' pattern out
of audio.js, update-banner.js, and main.js. Behaviour is unchanged;
the on-screen diagnostic label still clips to the same length.
Also drop { once: true } on the update-banner dismiss listener — if the
banner is ever reshown in a session the listener wouldn't refire.
refactor(scanner): load animations as ES modules
The two animation modules used IIFE + window.PexesongyAnim, with a
classic <script> tag dynamically injected at boot from inside the
<head>. The timing was racy (the classic script could land mid-load
of main.js) and the inline shim in index.html duplicated the storage
helper.
Switch animations/{rays,plane}.js to 'export default' modules and have
main.js dynamically import the chosen one via top-level await. The
inline loader in index.html disappears, the animation contract becomes
explicit, and main.js no longer threads a getter through to the
playback overlay.
refactor(ci): extract recipient encryption into testable script
The release-publisher dispatch step previously inlined a multi-line
Python heredoc inside a shell heredoc inside YAML to do AES-GCM
encryption — readable but unreviewable, and not testable. Move the
logic into scripts/encrypt_recipients.py with full pytest coverage of
parse rules and a round-trip decrypt check. release.yml shrinks by
~40 lines and becomes obvious. Also adds a clarifying comment that
scanner/apk-version.json is APK-only and not part of the Pages deploy.
cryptography becomes a poetry dev dependency so 'poetry run python
scripts/encrypt_recipients.py' works out of the box on CI.
fix(catalog): raise BrokenCardError instead of silent skip
A song-ready row missing front.png, meta.yaml, or youtube_id silently
disappeared from the loaded deck, turning typos into 'where'd that card
go' debugging. Raise BrokenCardError so build.py and extract_audio.py
fail loudly and immediately.
Cards in 'planned' / 'skipped' status are unaffected.

fix(android): scale launcher icon to 66% to stop OS-shape clipping
The cover-image launcher icon filled the full canvas, so on Android
8+ the device shape mask (circle / squircle / round-square) was
clipping the artwork's edges. Now the cover renders at 66% of the
canvas — well inside the 66dp adaptive safe zone — with a transparent
margin in the foreground and a black-with-rounded-corner card in the
legacy fallback. Whole drawing visible on every launcher shape.
feat(android): launcher icon = data/cover.png
Use the existing card-back cover as the app icon. Resampled into the
five mipmap densities (mdpi…xxxhdpi) for both legacy ic_launcher and
adaptive foreground. The previous attempt to bake a bespoke
"musical-notes-on-a-card" icon via PIL primitives didn't read well at
small launcher sizes; cover.png is recognisable, on-brand, and what
players actually see when they pick up a face-down card.
Removes the bespoke generator scripts (build_app_icon, contact-sheet)
since one ad-hoc PIL pipeline + a future regen instruction in the
README beats keeping a half-finished tool around.
feat(scripts): launcher-icon generator + contact sheet
build_app_icon.py renders 20 candidate launcher icons under
data/icon-candidates/ (gitignored). Each is a 1024² PNG combining a
light card silhouette (rounded / scalloped / beveled), a background
(black / warm / accent), and a music motif (single quaver, beamed
pair, triple sixteenth, treble clef) drawn with PIL primitives so
no font dependency at install time.
Pass --emit NN to resample a chosen variant into the full Android
mipmap-* set (mdpi through xxxhdpi) plus the adaptive-icon
foreground.
icon_contact_sheet.py composes a single 4×5 review sheet
(_sheet.png) so the candidates can be compared at a glance without
opening 20 files.
docs: top-level README
ci: re-include scanner/art in freshness diff + byte-stability test
Pillow is pinned via poetry.lock so PNG bytes match between local and
CI runs; verified: same input + same Pillow version produce
byte-identical optimized PNGs. Re-include scanner/art/ in the
freshness diff so a missed `build.py` run before commit is now caught
on every PR, not only when cards.json itself shifted.
Add a pytest test that runs scanner/build.py twice and asserts
cards.json is byte-stable across runs. Skipped when scanner/audio/
is empty (fresh clone case). Future non-deterministic edits to
build.py (timestamps, set iteration, etc.) fail this test loudly
rather than producing silent CI flake.
Tighten the "pre-sync" workflow comment so it's clear the version
extraction must run before `cap sync` copies scanner/ into the
Android assets dir.
refactor(scanner): split playback overlay, harden audio cache, dedupe SVGs
scanner/lib/playback-overlay.js (new)
  * Owns the AR-pose math (cardScreenCorners + deferred resolution),
    the homography-driven card overlay (showCardOnCorners), and the
    glide-to-centre animation. main.js drops to 289 lines doing only
    DOM wiring and orchestration.
  * Module exposes a factory createPlaybackOverlay({scene, reticle,
    cardFocusEl, cardFocusImg, anim}) — explicit dependencies, no
    coupling to specific DOM ids.
scanner/lib/audio.js
  * Reject downloads where blob.size disagrees with Content-Length,
    or where the response is suspiciously small (<1 KB — almost
    certainly an error page). Without this, a flaky network would
    write a truncated mp3 to the Filesystem cache and play half the
    song forever with no way to invalidate.
scanner/lib/main.js
  * loadCards() now distinguishes fetch / HTTP / parse / shape errors
    and the start-button error path uses a `phase` variable so a
    stale cards.json shows "Could not load cards" instead of the
    misleading "Camera failed to start".
scanner/index.html
  * Five inline SVGs collapsed via a hidden <svg><symbol>…</symbol>
    block at the top of <body>. Each icon usage is now one line; new
    icons are a one-line addition.
fix(scripts): tighten edges across catalog + extract + bump + devserver
card_catalog.py
  * Centralize id normalization in `_normalize_id()` — coerces YAML
    int (`10`) and str (`'08'`) decodings to a 2-char zero-padded
    string. Plain unquoted `1` would have produced `data/cards/1-…`
    lookups; now safe.
  * cards.yaml: quote all 22 ids consistently (was: 20 quoted, 08/09
    unquoted because PyYAML 6 happens to keep them as strings).
extract_audio.py
  * Detect youtube_id changes via a `<id>-<slug>.mp3.yt` sidecar.
    Editing meta.yaml's youtube_id no longer leaves a stale mp3 in
    cache. Existing mp3s record their current yid on the next run
    without redownloading; --force still re-pulls everything.
  * Sidecar files are gitignored — per-machine state.
bump_version.py
  * Refuse to commit when the regex substitution produced no change,
    or when build.gradle has no working-tree diff against HEAD. Stops
    accidental empty-commit / duplicate-bump runs.
devserver.py
  * Detect missing TLS cert and print the exact `openssl req` recipe
    so the next reader is one paste away from a working dev server,
    instead of staring at FileNotFoundError.
print/generate.py + tests
  * Extract `expand_copies()` so the per-card copy-multiplication
    logic can be tested without invoking the full PDF pipeline. Five
    new tests cover identity, pairs, triples, empty input, and
    invalid input.
ci: gate release/publish on commit subject only
The previous commit accidentally triggered the dispatch step because
its body contained the strings 'release' and 'publish' inside square
brackets while explaining the artifact-quota issue. GitHub Actions
contains() inspects the full commit message including the body, so
the predicate matched and release-publisher fired a duplicate
notification for v0.1.10.
Switch to a precomputed flags step that reads only the first line
(commit subject) and exposes release / publish booleans as outputs.
The Generate release notes, Create Release, and Dispatch to
release-publisher steps now gate on those outputs, so prose mentions
of those words in commit bodies are safe.
ci: drop redundant artifact upload + prune old artifacts
The Upload artifacts step duplicated the APK + AAB into workflow
storage, which has its own quota — and the same files already live
forever as GitHub Release assets (and on release-publisher, when
[publish] runs). Storage quota was hit, breaking the build. Removed
the step.
Added a Prune step that keeps only the 3 newest workflow artifacts on
master pushes, mirroring prague-mhd-dashboard's setup. Defensive: if
any future step uploads artifacts again, they self-clean instead of
piling up.
style(print): drop unused Iterable import
pyproject.toml gains a poetry dev-dependencies group (pytest, ruff)
plus [tool.ruff] and [tool.pytest.ini_options] config blocks.
tests/ — 18 cases across:
  * test_card_ids.py        — parse_card_ids spec parser (ranges,
                                comma lists, edge cases, invalid input).
  * test_card_catalog.py    — iter_song_ready_cards + iter_all_cards
                                shape on the live data tree.
  * test_bump_version.py    — versionCode + versionName regexes match
                                the current build.gradle and round-trip
                                through a tmp file correctly.
CLAUDE-TODO.md rewritten as a current-state summary instead of the
stale phase-by-phase plan whose phases 1 and 4 had already shipped.
ci: lint + test + freshness check, deps via poetry
Adds three steps to the existing build job:
  * ruff check — catches style and import drift.
  * pytest tests — 18 tests covering parse_card_ids, iter_all_cards /
    iter_song_ready_cards integrity, and the bump_version regexes
    against the live build.gradle.
  * scanner build freshness — runs build.py and fails the job if
    cards.json or targets.compile-order.txt drifted from the committed
    copies. scanner/art/ is intentionally excluded — PIL's deflate is
    not byte-stable across Pillow versions, so diffing PNGs would
    produce spurious failures.
Dependencies are now installed via 'poetry install --no-interaction
--no-root' instead of an ad-hoc pip-install list. pyproject.toml is
the single source of truth; new deps reach CI without editing this
workflow.
build(android): release hardening + ApkUpdater single-flight
Release build:
  * minifyEnabled true + shrinkResources true. R8 keep rules in
    proguard-rules.pro preserve @CapacitorPlugin / @PluginMethod
    reflection targets and ApkUpdaterPlugin's public surface; verified
    by running ./gradlew assembleRelease (succeeds up to the signing
    step locally; CI signs).
  * keepattributes SourceFile,LineNumberTable so release crashes still
    map to source lines.
Privacy:
  * allowBackup="false" + Android 12+ data_extraction_rules.xml
    excluding all domains. The cached audio under DATA/audio/ may
    contain copyrighted mp3s; we don't want them auto-uploaded to
    Google Drive on user devices or copied during device-to-device
    transfer. Everything is re-downloadable on a fresh install.
ApkUpdaterPlugin:
  * Single-flight executor + AtomicBoolean — concurrent taps on the
    Install button no longer race onto the same update.apk.
  * conn.disconnect() in a finally block.
  * 200 MB content-length cap (advertised + accumulated) — a misbehaving
    server can't fill the cache to OOM.
  * Drop the Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= N guard around CLEAR_TASK; minSdk
    is 22 and the flag has been around since API 11.
Capacitor:
  * cap sync registered the @capacitor/filesystem plugin in
    capacitor.build.gradle and capacitor.settings.gradle (was declared
    in package.json but missing from gradle includes).
fix(scripts): retarget bump, broaden discovery, tighten extract
bump_version.py
  * Was bumping scanner/version.json (dead file, nothing read it).
  * Now bumps app/android/app/build.gradle versionCode + versionName,
    which is the actual APK version source CI exposes via apk-version.
  * Drops --no-verify (used to avoid pre-push hook recursion); replaced
    with PEXESONGY_BUMPING=1 env-var guard.
  * Commits only the gradle path so other staged changes don't leak in.
find_youtube.py
  * Regression fix: previously iterated only song-ready cards, but the
    only reason to run this is to find a YouTube id for a planned card.
    Now uses iter_all_cards() (new in card_catalog.py) and accepts
    --planned to filter, plus positional id list.
extract_audio.py
  * Drop --download-sections start trim. Scanner already seeks via
    audioEl.currentTime = card.start_seconds, so trimming at extract
    time meant editing meta.yaml's start_seconds left a stale-cached
    mp3.
  * Default --quality 5 (VBR ~128 kbps, ~3 MB) instead of 0 (~10 MB).
    Phone speaker can't tell; first-scan over cellular is 3× faster.
devserver.py
  * Auto-detect LAN IPs from the routing table instead of hardcoding
    192.168.0.152.
phone_debug.py
  * Default --port 9223 (matches the adb-forward workflow recorded in
    .claude/settings.local.json), accept --port and --match overrides.
card_catalog.py
  * New CatalogEntry + iter_all_cards() — lightweight view from
    cards.yaml only, no front.png / meta.yaml required.
scripts/import_cards.sh -> scripts/archive/
  * One-shot importer; ran once on 2026-04-17 and is kept for history.
build(scanner): regenerable cards.json + compile-order sentinel
scanner/build.py now also writes scanner/compile-input/ (numbered PNG
copies that the user uploads to the MindAR browser compiler) and
records the upload manifest in scanner/targets.compile-order.txt.
Subsequent runs diff the manifest and warn when targets.mind needs to
be recompiled — eliminates the silent index-mismatch failure mode
(audio plays for the wrong card after a recompile in different order).
cards.json is slimmer: dropped the redundant `youtube_id` field
(scanner reads only `audio` / `audio_url` now) and the duplicated
`compiled_reversed` / `target_images` debug fields.
Other changes:
  * build.sh writes scanner/build-info.json (sha+date) for the CF Pages
    deploy. The committed scanner/version.json (which nothing read) is
    deleted; the APK version lives in app/android/app/build.gradle.
  * PUBLIC_BASE_URL constant — was duplicated 17× per-card-row.
  * audio_url is mandatory (build.py raises if mp3 missing) so the
    scanner can drop its multi-step fallback chain.
refactor(scanner): split into ES modules, drop dead YouTube IFrame code
The single 1035-line inline IIFE in index.html became 305 lines + five
focused ES modules under scanner/lib/. Removed the YouTube IFrame Player
plumbing entirely (~150 lines): every card has had a local mp3 for some
time, the YT path was unreachable but still executing fragile lifecycle
code (player creation, ytReady tracking, pendingAudio queue) on every
boot, and would NPE if YT.PlayerState was ever read after the iframe
script tag was removed.
New layout:
  lib/storage.js       — try/catch wrapper around localStorage
  lib/homography.js    — quad→quad math + matrix3d helper
  lib/audio.js         — playback + Capacitor Filesystem cache
  lib/update-banner.js — APK version compare + ApkUpdater bridge
  lib/main.js          — DOM wiring + AR lifecycle
Side fixes:
  * Drop dead 'card.art ||' fallback (build.py always populates it).
  * Use optional chaining for window.Capacitor probes consistently.
  * Surface anim start() errors via the diagnostic version label
    instead of silently swallowing them.

feat(android): launcher icon = data/cover.png
Use the existing card-back cover as the app icon. Resampled into the
five mipmap densities (mdpi…xxxhdpi) for both legacy ic_launcher and
adaptive foreground. The previous attempt to bake a bespoke
"musical-notes-on-a-card" icon via PIL primitives didn't read well at
small launcher sizes; cover.png is recognisable, on-brand, and what
players actually see when they pick up a face-down card.
Removes the bespoke generator scripts (build_app_icon, contact-sheet)
since one ad-hoc PIL pipeline + a future regen instruction in the
README beats keeping a half-finished tool around.
feat(scripts): launcher-icon generator + contact sheet
build_app_icon.py renders 20 candidate launcher icons under
data/icon-candidates/ (gitignored). Each is a 1024² PNG combining a
light card silhouette (rounded / scalloped / beveled), a background
(black / warm / accent), and a music motif (single quaver, beamed
pair, triple sixteenth, treble clef) drawn with PIL primitives so
no font dependency at install time.
Pass --emit NN to resample a chosen variant into the full Android
mipmap-* set (mdpi through xxxhdpi) plus the adaptive-icon
foreground.
icon_contact_sheet.py composes a single 4×5 review sheet
(_sheet.png) so the candidates can be compared at a glance without
opening 20 files.

fix(scanner): drop YouTube IFrame entirely + close diagonal seam in plane tiles [release] [publish]
Two more fixes from screenshot:
- Removed <script src=youtube/iframe_api>. Every card has a local mp3,
  so the YT player was unused dead weight that kept leaking a giant
  play overlay onto the scan view (despite earlier attempts to hide
  it). Player init guarded by ytReady which now stays false forever.
  If a YT-only card ever appears in cards.json we'll bring the script
  back; until then, no overlay.
- The cover image on each tile was rendered as two affine-mapped
  triangles. The shared diagonal showed a 1px black seam from
  sub-pixel rounding. Outset each triangle's clip by 1px along the
  outward normal so the two triangles overlap a hair across the
  diagonal — hides the seam.

fix(scanner): hide stray YouTube play overlay; force camera fullscreen [release] [publish]
Two visual bugs reported on v0.1.8 with screenshot:
1. The YouTube IFrame host was 200×200 at top:-220px, supposed to be
   off-screen, but Android Chrome was bringing the giant play
   overlay back into view. Reduce to a 1×1 px host with opacity
   0.01 + iframe sized to 1px so the overlay can't render at any
   visible size while still passing autoplay heuristics.
2. MindAR's <video> rendered in a small box top-left instead of
   filling the screen, because the WebView's clientWidth/Height
   reading mid-launch was off. Add !important position/size CSS so
   the camera always covers the viewport regardless of MindAR's
   computed offsets.

scan mode. Add a CSS opacity transition on body > video so it ramps
0 → 1 over 1 second after MindAR starts. UI elements (home icon,
hint, reticle) appear immediately at full opacity above the fading
camera. Reset on exitScanBtn so re-entering re-fades.

camera. Reset on exitScanBtn so re-entering re-fades.
perf: ship 512² fronts + 256² cover, drop canvas DPR cap [release] [publish]
Two changes that compound:
- scanner/build.py now downsizes data/ → scanner/art/ via Pillow
  Lanczos. Originals at 1024² stay in data/ for print PDFs (printed
  card quality unaffected). scanner/art/ shrinks 46MB → 9.6MB.
  Card fronts: 512², ~530KB each. Cover: 256², ~107KB.
- scanner/animations/plane.js: DPR cap was already 1, kept that way.
  Each textured-tile draw now samples a 256² source instead of 1024²,
  a 16× cheaper texture sample, and the canvas backing buffer is
  4× smaller than at native DPR.
Net: plane animation should run smoothly during the 5s glide on
mid-range phones. APK shrinks correspondingly.

refactor: rename pin → calibration, fix calibrate-from-frame propagation [fw-release]
Rename: the word "pin" was unclear (overlap with PIN codes etc).
End-to-end rename to "calibration" — the same word users already see
in the UI:
- Worker: CameraPin → Calibration. cameraPin field → calibration.
  encodeCameraPin → encodeCalibration. calibrationToPin →
  telemetryToCalibration. Cue header X-FridgEye-Cam-Pin →
  X-FridgEye-Cam-Calibration.
- Firmware: cam_pin.c → calibration.c. cam_pin_t → calibration_t.
  cam_pin_load/save/clear/parse → calibration_load/save/clear/parse.
  s_active_pin → s_active_calibration. s_have_pin →
  s_have_calibration. camera_apply_pin → camera_apply_calibration.
  upload_result_t.cam_pin_encoded → calibration_encoded.
- App: data class CameraPin → Calibration. cameraPin → calibration.
NVS key value stays "cam_pin_v1" for backward compatibility — already-
calibrated devices keep their stored values across the upgrade.
Propagation fix: decorateCues was sending both the calibrate cue AND
the cameraPin header during calibrate-from-frame. The firmware
processed the calibrate cue first (switching the sensor to AUTO,
clearing local state), then the pin header (re-pinning). Effect:
benign but wasteful — and conceptually wrong: a server already
holding the values shouldn't ask the device to "calibrate". Now the
two paths are split:
- Server has values → ship X-FridgEye-Cam-Calibration only.
- Server staged a calibrate cue with no values → ship
  X-FridgEye-Cmd-Calibrate only.
Bumps firmware 0.7.2, app 0.9.3.
feat: calibration ack flow + snackbar feedback + single-button card
Worker now tracks "pin set but not yet confirmed by device" properly:
- calibrate-from-frame writes the pin AND sets calibratePending=true.
  The flag clears only when the device's next telemetry reports
  aecValue + agcGain matching the pin (path 2 of
  applyCalibrationFromTelemetry, alongside the existing regular
  calibrate flow).
- Cue decoration suppresses the X-FridgEye-Cam-Pin header once the
  device's reported values already match the stored pin — no point
  re-shipping bytes a calibrated device has already applied. Same
  for X-FridgEye-Cmd-Calibrate.
App settings card collapses to one button per state:
- "Auto calibration" + Calibrate button.
- "Calibration awaiting device confirmation" + Remove button.
- "Calibration set" + AEC/AGC/LDR rows + Remove button.
Recalibrate / Unlock duplicated Calibrate / Remove for no reason;
dropped.
History swipe-right now snackbars the result:
- Success: "Calibration sent — awaiting device confirmation".
- 409 (frame too dark, missing metadata): surfaced from the
  Worker's body verbatim.
- Other failures: error message.
ViewModel exposes a Channel-backed events flow; snackbar host lives
on the Scaffold.
Bumps to 0.9.1.

fix(app): history viewer pan stays edge-to-edge, no inland corners
Pan now constrained per-axis so the image's edges either sit at the
matching screen edge or off-screen — never floating inland with
black on the other side. On the long axis (e.g. vertical for a 4:3
photo on a tall phone) letterboxing stays at scale=1; once zoom
exceeds what fills that axis, the bars disappear and pan is
constrained too.
Image is now explicitly sized to the rendered base dimensions
(boxSize × effective aspect, with width/height swapped at 90°/270°
so the rotation transform lands at the right visual size) instead
of relying on ContentScale.Fit on a fillMaxSize Image. Without
this, rotation transforms left the image at its original aspect,
shrinking it inside the screen — same root cause as the inland
edges the user noticed.
Bumps to 0.8.2.
feat(app): history viewer seeds saved rotation + transient rotate button
Opening a history frame now applies the device's saved rotation
(seeded from DataStore's first emission, same race-fix pattern as
the main card) so the photo is upright on entry. A rotate icon in
the topbar cycles 0/90/180/270 for ad-hoc fixes — strictly local,
discarded on back, never persisted. Saving rotation as the device
default still lives on the main card.
Quarter-turns swap which axis fills the screen so a 4:3 frame
rotated 90° still touches both screen edges with no letterbox on
the long axis. Pan/zoom reset when the user rotates because the
pan vector points into a different part of the image afterward.
Bumps to 0.8.1.
feat: camera exposure pin + dark-frame skip + fullscreen history viewer [fw-release]
Device side:
- New cam_pin module (NVS-backed) holds AEC value + AGC gain + LDR
  baseline. Applied on every boot after camera_init, before the first
  capture, so a calibrated device always uses the same exposure.
- camera_capture reads back s->status.aec_value / agc_gain + LDR raw
  per frame; values land in the multipart telemetry JSON.
- is_low_light(ldr_raw, pin) decides per frame: pre-calibration uses
  the global FRIDGEYE_LDR_THRESHOLD_RAW (kconfig now common to both
  builds); post-calibration uses pin.ldr_baseline / 2. Dark frames
  skip the JPEG upload entirely and ping /status instead, so the
  device stays "online" without overwriting the latest good frame.
- New cue headers: X-FridgEye-Cmd-Calibrate (one-shot) and
  X-FridgEye-Cam-Pin "aec,agc,ldrBaseline" (always-on while a pin
  exists). Firmware persists the pin and re-applies on the next
  capture; no reboot needed.
- ldr_read_raw moved out of battery.c into a USB-buildable ldr.c TU
  so both builds can sample without dragging in the rest of battery.h.
- Firmware bumped to 0.7.0 (lockstep with the Worker constant — the
  version-sync CI gate enforces this).
Worker side:
- CameraPin stored on the KV DeviceRecord; POST /calibrate stages
  the cue, DELETE /calibrate clears both pending state and any
  existing pin. Calibration result is captured when a /upload or
  /status response carries aecValue+agcGain+ldrRaw AND the LDR raw
  is bright enough (MIN_CALIBRATION_LDR_RAW=1200) — refuses to pin
  from a dim frame so the user gets a clean retry on the next try.
- Telemetry validators added for aecValue, agcGain, ldrRaw,
  lowLight, skippedDarkFrames. All optional, all range-checked.
App side:
- DeviceTelemetry + DeviceSummary gain the new fields. Camera
  exposure card on settings: Calibrate / Recalibrate / Unlock with
  the pinned values shown as InfoRows. UI flips immediately on
  request (refresh-after-dispatch) so the user sees "pending" without
  waiting for the next watch tick.
- History strip honours the saved zoom/rotation so frames render the
  same way as the main card.
- Tapping a history frame opens a new fullscreen viewer: no
  rotation applied, fit-to-width so the long axis hits both screen
  edges, pinch-zoom (1x..8x) + drag-pan, translucent topbar with
  the local capture time. Zoom state is discarded on back — by
  design, history is read-only.
- App bumped to 0.8.0.
feat(worker): camera-pin + calibrate cue + dark-frame telemetry
Adds the server side of "lock the camera exposure once, skip dark
frames":
- New CameraPin (AEC value + AGC gain + LDR baseline + capturedAt)
  stored on the KV DeviceRecord. Always shipped back on upload/status
  responses as X-FridgEye-Cam-Pin so the firmware persists+applies
  it. Encoded as "aec,agc,ldrBaseline" for a cheap on-device parse.
- POST /devices/:id/calibrate (Google-authed) sets a sticky cue. The
  device picks it up via X-FridgEye-Cmd-Calibrate, runs AEC normally
  on the next capture, and reports converged values in the same
  upload's telemetry JSON (aecValue, agcGain, ldrRaw). Worker turns
  that into a pin, clears the cue. Refuses calibrations from a dim
  frame (ldrRaw < 1200) so a fridge-bulb-off capture doesn't pin
  to garbage.
- DELETE /devices/:id/calibrate clears both pending state and any
  existing pin — the UI "Unlock" action.
- Telemetry gains aecValue, agcGain, ldrRaw, lowLight,
  skippedDarkFrames. All optional, all validated; AWB stays auto
  since the OV2640 status struct doesn't expose per-channel gains.
Firmware + app land in follow-ups.

feat: per-device history of last 10 frames
Worker keeps a ring buffer at users/<sub>/<id>/history/<ms>.jpg, written
alongside the existing latest.jpg on every multipart upload. Two new
endpoints serve the app: GET /devices/:id/history (timestamps,
newest-first) and GET /devices/:id/history/<ts>.jpg (one frame).
History is wiped together with the rest of the device record on
unlink confirm.
App gets a History card on the device settings screen → a vertical
list of frames with timestamps. Motivates the "is the dark frame just
the door closing?" diagnostic — the trend across the last 10 frames
makes that obvious.
Bumps to 0.7.0.

fix(app): seed photo zoom/rotation from DataStore, not async initial
Saved rotation never re-appeared on next launch. The LaunchedEffect that
seeded liveRotation read savedZoom on first composition, but
collectAsStateWithLifecycle emits its initialValue (Identity, rotation=0)
synchronously and only later replaces it with the DataStore value. The
`seeded` gate then locked in the 0 and ignored the real emission.
Seed by collecting the flow's first() directly inside the effect so the
DataStore value is awaited before assignment.
Bumps to 0.6.6.

release(app): v0.6.5 — hide rotate pill in saved state
Releases the UX fix from 5ddff68 that didn't make v0.6.4. The
rotate pill is now overlay-only — visible together with Reset /
Save when the live view differs from the saved zoom/rotation,
hidden in the steady-state card. Discoverability tradeoff: a user
with a sideways-mounted device has to pinch to zoom first (or
otherwise enter the "differs" state) before they see the rotate
control. Accepted; a tap-to-edit-mode flow is the v0.7 polish if
the discoverability hole becomes a real complaint.
App 0.6.5 / versionCode 42. Worker + firmware stay at 0.6.4.
fix(app): hide rotate pill in saved state, same as Reset/Save
The previous commit (4dca173) shipped the rotate pill as
always-visible whenever a bitmap rendered — the intent was
discoverability for users with a sideways-mounted camera. But that
trades one problem for another: the photo card looks cluttered in
its normal (saved) state, with a control nobody needs after the
initial mount adjustment.
Going with the cleaner default: rotate is visible only when the
live view differs from saved, same trigger as the Reset / Save
pills. A user with a sideways photo discovers rotation by pinching
to zoom (which puts them into the "differs-from-saved" state),
seeing all three pills, and tapping rotate. Indirect, but the card
stays clean in steady state.
Accepted limitation: a user who never pinches will never discover
rotation. If that becomes a real complaint, the v0.7 fix is a tap-
to-edit-mode flow where any single tap on the photo reveals all
three pills for a few seconds. Not building it yet.
Not bumping app version — this rides with the next user-facing
release. v0.6.4 (currently rolling out) keeps the always-visible
rotate; users who upgrade past it land on the cleaner default.

feat(worker): camera-pin + calibrate cue + dark-frame telemetry
Adds the server side of "lock the camera exposure once, skip dark
frames":
- New CameraPin (AEC value + AGC gain + LDR baseline + capturedAt)
  stored on the KV DeviceRecord. Always shipped back on upload/status
  responses as X-FridgEye-Cam-Pin so the firmware persists+applies
  it. Encoded as "aec,agc,ldrBaseline" for a cheap on-device parse.
- POST /devices/:id/calibrate (Google-authed) sets a sticky cue. The
  device picks it up via X-FridgEye-Cmd-Calibrate, runs AEC normally
  on the next capture, and reports converged values in the same
  upload's telemetry JSON (aecValue, agcGain, ldrRaw). Worker turns
  that into a pin, clears the cue. Refuses calibrations from a dim
  frame (ldrRaw < 1200) so a fridge-bulb-off capture doesn't pin
  to garbage.
- DELETE /devices/:id/calibrate clears both pending state and any
  existing pin — the UI "Unlock" action.
- Telemetry gains aecValue, agcGain, ldrRaw, lowLight,
  skippedDarkFrames. All optional, all validated; AWB stays auto
  since the OV2640 status struct doesn't expose per-channel gains.
Firmware + app land in follow-ups.
feat: per-device history of last 10 frames
Worker keeps a ring buffer at users/<sub>/<id>/history/<ms>.jpg, written
alongside the existing latest.jpg on every multipart upload. Two new
endpoints serve the app: GET /devices/:id/history (timestamps,
newest-first) and GET /devices/:id/history/<ts>.jpg (one frame).
History is wiped together with the rest of the device record on
unlink confirm.
App gets a History card on the device settings screen → a vertical
list of frames with timestamps. Motivates the "is the dark frame just
the door closing?" diagnostic — the trend across the last 10 frames
makes that obvious.
Bumps to 0.7.0.
